fields.description: Understanding core medical concepts, anatomy, physiology, pathology, and evidence-based knowledge

fields.description: Complete diagnostic process from history taking and physical examination to formulating final diagnosis

fields.description: Treatment planning, implementation, prevention strategies, and medical/surgical interventions

fields.name: Patient Care: Prevention
fields.description: Health maintenance, disease prevention strategies, patient counseling on individualized health strategies
Understand and apply the foundational science principles of epidemiology, pathophysiology, and pathology for common internal medicine conditions including medical emergencies.

Conduct a comprehensive history and physical examination, select appropriate laboratory and diagnostic studies, formulate accurate differential diagnoses and determine diagnosis and prognoses for patients with common internal medicine conditions and medical emergencies.

Formulate initial patient-centered management plans including pharmacotherapy, clinical interventions, health maintenance, disease prevention, and emergency management, while considering the risks, benefits, and alternatives for each intervention.
Use evidence-based medicine to solve clinical problems and engage in lifelong self-learning activities to improve patient care
Demonstrate effective communication and interpersonal skills with patients, their families, and healthcare team and collaborate effectively with other colleagues.

Demonstrate adherence professional and ethical guidelines during all interactions with patients, their families and healthcare team in all aspects of the rotation Marks will be deducted for un-excused absences in accordance with the attendance policy.

Understand and apply the foundational science principles of epidemiology, pathophysiology, and pathology for complex internal medicine conditions and medical emergencies, including patients with comorbidities or requiring subspecialty consultation.

Independently conduct comprehensive history and physical exams on complex patients, interpret advanced diagnostic studies, refine differential diagnoses list, and determine diagnoses and prognoses for a broad range of internal medicine conditions and emergencies, considering comorbidities, need for critical care or subspecialty consultation.

Formulate, implement, follow up, and adjust patient-centered management plans that include pharmacotherapy, clinical interventions, emergency management, health maintenance, and disease prevention, while considering risks, benefits, and alternatives.

Recognize the role of healthcare systems in patient care, advocate for patient safety, and apply principles of cost-effective, high-quality care while navigating interdisciplinary teams and healthcare resources
Demonstrate effective communication and interpersonal skills with patients, their families, and healthcare team and collaborate effectively with other colleagues.

Demonstrate adherence to professional and ethical guidelines during all interactions with patients, their families and healthcare team in all aspects of the rotation. Marks will be deducted for un-excused absences in accordance with the attendance policy.

Understand and apply the foundational science principles of epidemiology, pathophysiology, pathology, clinical anatomy, embryology, and genetics for common disorders in obstetrics and gynecology including emergencies

Conduct a comprehensive history and physical examination, select appropriate laboratory and diagnostic studies, formulate accurate differential diagnoses and determine provisional diagnosis and prognoses for patients with various disorders in obstetrics and gynecology.

Formulate initial patient-centered management plans including clinical interventions, pharmacotherapy, and emergency management of various disorders in obstetrics and gynecology, while considering the risks, benefits, and alternatives for each intervention.

Understand the principle of counselling women on individualized health maintenance strategies, incorporating screening, prevention, education, and surveillance appropriate for their specific life stage and risk factors.
Demonstrate effective communication and interpersonal skills to build empathic relationships with patients and their families, and collaborate effectively with healthcare teams.

Demonstrate adherence to professional and ethical guidelines during all interactions with patients, their families and healthcare team in all aspects of the rotation Marks will be deducted for un-excused absences in accordance with the attendance policy.
Use evidence-based medicine to solve clinical problems and engage in lifelong self-learning activities to improve patient care and become up to date in obstetrics and gynecology

Understand and apply the foundational science principles of epidemiology, pathophysiology, pathology, clinical anatomy, embryology, and genetics for common disorders in obstetrics and gynecology including emergencies and advanced clinical correlation of basic science in clinical practice.

Independently and professionally conduct comprehensive history and physical exams, interpret advanced diagnostic studies, refine differential diagnoses list, and determine diagnoses and prognoses for various obstetrics and gynecology disorders including emergencies, considering the need for critical care or subspecialty consultation.

Develop and implement follow up strategies and adjust patient-centered management plans for various obstetrics and gynecology disorders, including clinical interventions, pharmacotherapy, and emergency management, considering risks, benefits, and offering alternatives in treatment including conservative, medical, and surgical options.

Professionally counsel women on individualized health maintenance strategies, incorporating screening, prevention, education, and surveillance appropriate for their specific life stage and risk factors. Discuss the pros and cons of various treatment strategies allowing shared discussion and decision with the patient.

Demonstrate effective communication and interpersonal skills to build empathic relationships with patients and their families and collaborate effectively with healthcare teams. Realizing the importance of building relationships to achieve professional success.

Adhere to professional and ethical guidelines during all clinical interactions with patients, their families and healthcare team by exhibiting integrity and respect for patient confidentiality and autonomy and adhere to the highest professional behavior with healthcare team and colleagues. Marks will be deducted for un-excused absences in accordance with the attendance policy.

Understand and apply the foundational sciences, including normal growth, development, and behavior from infancy through adolescence, as well as principles of physiology, pathophysiology, and pharmacology.

Conduct a comprehensive pediatric history taking, physical examination while adapting appropriately to the age of the patient, interpret clinical data, formulate a differential diagnosis, determine the diagnosis, and identify associated risks.
Formulate patient-centered management plans for pediatric conditions, including pharmacotherapy, clinical interventions, health maintenance, and preventive care.
Use evidence-based medicine to solve clinical problems and engage in lifelong self-learning.
Demonstrate effective communication and interpersonal skills to build empathic relationships with children and their families, and collaborate effectively with healthcare teams.

Demonstrate professional and ethical behavior during interactions with patients, families, and the healthcare team by respecting patient and family attitudes, behaviors, and lifestyles, and adapting care to cultural, ethnic, and socioeconomic influences. Marks will be deducted for un-excused absences in accordance with the attendance policy.

fields.name: Clinical Examination — Cardiovascular System
fields.scenario: A 62-year-old female is referred for evaluation of a cardiac murmur detected during a routine check-up. Perform a complete cardiovascular examination.
fields.instructions: Perform a systematic cardiovascular examination on the standardized patient. Narrate your findings as you examine.

fields.name: Emergency Scenario — PPH Management
fields.scenario: A 32-year-old woman delivered vaginally 30 minutes ago. She is now bleeding heavily (estimated blood loss 1000 mL) and becoming tachycardic. Manage this postpartum hemorrhage.
fields.instructions: Demonstrate your systematic approach to managing primary PPH. Use the mannequin and state your actions clearly.

fields.name: History Taking — Chest Pain
fields.scenario: A 55-year-old male presents to the Emergency Department with acute onset chest pain radiating to the left arm for the past 2 hours. He is diaphoretic and anxious. Take a focused history from the patient.
fields.instructions: You have 7 minutes to take a focused history from the standardized patient. Cover all relevant aspects of the presenting complaint.

fields.name: Emergency Scenario — PPH Management
fields.scenario: A 32-year-old woman delivered vaginally 30 minutes ago. She is now bleeding heavily (estimated blood loss 1000 mL) and becoming tachycardic. Manage this postpartum hemorrhage.
fields.instructions: Demonstrate your systematic approach to managing primary PPH. Use the mannequin and state your actions clearly.

fields.name: Surgical Examination — Abdominal Mass
fields.scenario: A 65-year-old female presents with a palpable mass in the right hypochondrium discovered incidentally. Perform a focused abdominal examination.
fields.instructions: Perform a systematic surgical abdominal examination focusing on the palpable mass. Demonstrate all relevant clinical signs.

fields.name: History Taking — Chest Pain
fields.scenario: A 55-year-old male presents to the Emergency Department with acute onset chest pain radiating to the left arm for the past 2 hours. He is diaphoretic and anxious. Take a focused history from the patient.
fields.instructions: You have 7 minutes to take a focused history from the standardized patient. Cover all relevant aspects of the presenting complaint.

fields.name: Neonatal Resuscitation Scenario
fields.scenario: You are called to attend the delivery of a term infant born via emergency C-section for fetal distress. At birth, the infant is floppy, not breathing, heart rate 80 bpm. Demonstrate your neonatal resuscitation approach.
fields.instructions: Using the neonatal mannequin, demonstrate the steps of neonatal resuscitation following NRP guidelines.

fields.name: Surgical Instrument Identification
fields.scenario: You are shown 7 commonly used surgical instruments. Identify each instrument, describe its use, and state in which surgical procedure it is commonly employed.
fields.instructions: Identify the instruments displayed, describe their function, and mention clinical applications.

fields.name: ECG Interpretation
fields.scenario: You are given a 12-lead ECG of a 68-year-old patient who presented with palpitations and dizziness. Interpret the ECG systematically and provide your diagnosis.
fields.instructions: Interpret the ECG provided. State your approach, identify abnormalities, and provide a clinical correlation.

fields.name: CTG Interpretation
fields.scenario: A 34-week pregnant woman is on continuous CTG monitoring due to reduced fetal movements. You are shown a 20-minute CTG trace. Interpret the trace and recommend management.
fields.instructions: Interpret the CTG trace using the DR C BRAVADO systematic approach. Classify the trace and recommend management.

fields.name: Clinical Examination — Obstetric Examination
fields.scenario: A 30-year-old multigravida at 36 weeks gestation presents for routine assessment. Perform a complete obstetric abdominal examination.
fields.instructions: Perform a systematic obstetric abdominal examination on the mannequin/standardized patient. Determine fetal lie, presentation, and engagement.

fields.name: Post-operative Management Scenario
fields.scenario: A 55-year-old male underwent open cholecystectomy 12 hours ago. He is now tachycardic (HR 110), febrile (38.2°C), hypotensive (BP 90/60), with decreased urine output. Assess and manage this post-operative complication.
fields.instructions: Assess the patient, identify the likely complication, and outline your immediate management plan.

fields.name: Clinical Examination — Cardiovascular System
fields.scenario: A 62-year-old female is referred for evaluation of a cardiac murmur detected during a routine check-up. Perform a complete cardiovascular examination.
fields.instructions: Perform a systematic cardiovascular examination on the standardized patient. Narrate your findings as you examine.

fields.name: History Taking — Antenatal Visit
fields.scenario: A 28-year-old primigravida at 32 weeks gestation presents for her routine antenatal visit. She reports mild headache and swollen ankles for 2 days. Take a focused antenatal history.
fields.instructions: Take a comprehensive antenatal history from the standardized patient. Identify risk factors and red flags.

fields.name: Growth Chart Interpretation
fields.scenario: You are given the growth chart of a 2-year-old girl brought for a well-child visit. She was born at term with a birth weight of 3.2 kg. Review the growth trajectory and identify any concerns.
fields.instructions: Interpret the growth chart provided. Identify the growth pattern, flag abnormalities, and suggest an approach.

fields.name: History Taking — Febrile Child
fields.scenario: A 3-year-old child is brought to the pediatric ER by his mother with high fever (39.5°C) for 3 days, irritability, and decreased oral intake. Take a focused pediatric history.
fields.instructions: Take a comprehensive pediatric history from the mother. Cover all age-appropriate aspects.

fields.name: Data Interpretation (Labs + Imaging)
fields.scenario: A 50-year-old diabetic patient is admitted with fatigue, peripheral edema, and reduced urine output. Review the provided laboratory results (CBC, BMP, urinalysis) and chest X-ray, then provide your interpretation and management plan.
fields.instructions: Interpret the lab results and imaging provided. Identify abnormalities, formulate a diagnosis, and outline initial management.

fields.instructions: Interpret the CTG trace using the DR C BRAVADO systematic approach. Classify the trace and recommend management.
